findings.explanation: set_fee_bps does not require the caller to be the config authority, so any signer can set arbitrary fees and redirect protocol economics.
delegate_withdraw accepts the owner as an unchecked AccountInfo and never requires owner.is_signer, allowing anyone to withdraw from another user's vault.

withdraw trusts a caller-supplied vault_authority without enforcing the expected PDA seeds, allowing an attacker-controlled authority to sign token transfers from attacker-owned token accounts while crediting victim accounting.
pay invokes a caller-supplied token_program instead of Program<Token>, allowing malicious CPI code to run with the provided writable accounts.
deposit never checks the user_token and vault_token mints or authorities, so users can deposit worthless tokens and receive credit as if they deposited the protocol mint.
accrue_rewards uses wrapping_add and unchecked multiplication, letting large elapsed time or reward rate wrap rewards to an incorrect value.
initialize_pool uses init_if_needed and overwrites authority and mint fields without an initialized guard, allowing an existing pool to be reconfigured by another signer.
close_position sends the closed account lamports to an arbitrary receiver instead of the position owner, allowing anyone who controls close conditions to redirect rent refunds.
sweep_remaining_rewards iterates over remaining_accounts and mutates any writable account without validating ownership, type, or relationship to the reward pool.
borrow reads the oracle price without checking last_update_slot freshness, enabling loans against stale collateral prices.
claim_refund transfers lamports to an arbitrary recipient instead of the order buyer, allowing the signer to redirect refunds away from the rightful owner.
create_profile derives a PDA from two variable-length byte strings without separators or length prefixes, so distinct username/domain pairs can collide to the same seed bytes.
